response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack software craftsman (TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, web/mobile/embedded) but has no demonstrated experience in the core requirements of this Principal Performance Architect role: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or hardware-software co-design. The brief C/C++ work at National Instruments and a wave-field-synthesis thesis are the only hardware-adjacent touchpoints, and they are far from the deep silicon performance-architecture expertise required. While the bachelor's-plus-experience educational path is technically met, the substantive skill mismatch makes this candidate unsuitable for a principal-level IC role in this specialized domain.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career with low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Some systems-level C/C++ exposure (NI LabWindows/CVI) and embedded/robotics work
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source platform leadership
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU architecture experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization background
Career is web/full-stack focused, not hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: Lack of SoC/IP modeling & hardware co-design
No AI accelerator / DNN optimization experience
Strong general SWE & high-performance systems background
